title: Non-overlapping Intervals
problem_statement: Given a list of intervals, return the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest non-overlapping.

examples.explanation: Remove [1,3] to eliminate overlap and keep the rest.

intuition: Greedily keep intervals with the earliest end time to maximize the number of non-overlapping intervals.

approaches.name: Greedy by end time
approaches.description: Sort intervals by end time, keep the current interval if it does not overlap, and count removals otherwise.
approaches.why_not: Brute force over subsets is exponential and not feasible for large n.

interview_qa.a: Earliest finishing intervals leave the most room for subsequent intervals.
An interval overlaps if its start is less than the last kept interval’s end.
